body: On the homepage, inspect whether the above-fold content communicates a sufficiently broad and accurate representation of the site's product catalog scope — specifically whether visitors can infer the range of product types available without scrolling. Positive signals include diverse product category imagery (not limited to a single gender, category, or promotional theme), clear text or navigation cues indicating the breadth of the assortment, and hero or grid content that avoids overrepresenting any single product line. Negative signals include a homepage dominated by a single product type, gender, or promotional campaign without cross-category visibility, or a hero section that scrolls users through sale items without surfacing the site's full catalog scope — both patterns lead first-time users to incorrectly assume unavailable product lines and exit prematurely. When violations are found, recommend diversifying featured product imagery to represent at least the major catalog segments, and supplement promotional hero content with persistent category navigation cues visible above the fold. Baymard Institute's ecommerce UX benchmark shows that 22% of sites showcase an inadequate range of products on the homepage, causing some users who don't see the product type they are looking for to assume it is not available on the site.

provenance.citation_text: First-time users on a site rely heavily on the main navigation options and the homepage content to determine which type of site they've landed on and how broad or narrow a product range it offers. However, Baymard's ecommerce UX benchmark shows that 22% of the sites showcase an inadequate range of products on the homepage. Some users who don't see the product type they are looking for will assume it is not available on the site — risking a preventable lost sale.

ai_review.reviewer_persona: neural-heuristic-reviewer v1.0 (top-1% senior CRO consultant; 20yr; multi-vertical D2C/SaaS/marketplace/B2B/lead-gen/fintech/media)
ai_review.why_generated: Drafter targeted Baymard's homepage-catalog-scope heuristic — first-time-user catalog inference is one of Baymard's signature ecommerce-UX-benchmark findings. Topic is well-positioned for the homepage page_type segment of T103.
ai_review.how_reviewed: URL pattern matches Baymard blog format (HTTP 200 verified by drafter); 22% inadequate-range stat sits within Baymard's documented site-compliance gap range (typically 15-40%); citation_text reads as authentic Baymard prose with characteristic 'ecommerce UX benchmark' framing; body recommendation (diversify above-fold imagery + persistent category nav) is concrete consultant-grade advice; manifest selectors correctly scoped to homepage + D2C/marketplace ecommerce.

dimension_findings.finding: URL https://baymard.com/blog/inferring-product-catalog-from-homepage matches Baymard blog format; HTTP 200 verified at draft time
22% inadequate-homepage-range stat within Baymard's typical 15-40% site-compliance-gap range; citation phrasing authentic to Baymard style
homepage-only correctly limited; D2C+marketplace appropriate for ecommerce focus; device array permissive (principle is device-agnostic)
'preventable lost sale' is descriptive observation; no conversion-rate predictions, no banned causal-claim phrasing
quantitative value=22 directly derivable from citation_text; metric label clear and methodologically defensible
recommendation specifies two concrete interventions (diversify featured imagery + persistent category nav above fold) — would ship verbatim in real consultant deliverable
